mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and lower equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic slowdown, which typically leads to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation combined with declining retail sales indicates stagflationary pressures, eroding consumer purchasing power and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 5% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized change < 0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where high inflation coincides with declining retail sales.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion, strong industrial production growth indicates healthy manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during periods of robust economic expansion.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic activity, leading to reduced investment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1000 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances where housing starts fall below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is aggressively raising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is simultaneously slowing or contracting, it signals an impending recession and significant downside risk for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.75pp in 3 months AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 1%
evidence_refs: fact:monetary policy tightening often precedes economic slowdowns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where aggressive rate hikes coincide with low GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, creates uncertainty about future monetary policy and erodes real earnings, leading to short-term negative sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances where CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 8%.
